Founder’s Note 01 / 01

“I’ve lived on both sides of the recovery line. I know the exact moment where everything we gained starts to slip — because no one was there to hold the system together.”

Michael Anthony Hines Founder & CEO, iRecover Services, LLC
The Foundation

Built on lived experience,
operational discipline, and faith.

Where Recovery Meets Opportunity.

iRecover Services exists because access to employment alone doesn’t produce lasting recovery. People returning to the workforce need a structured system of support — mentorship, accountability, stability, and dignity.

Michael Anthony Hines Founder & CEO

Michael Anthony Hines

CEO & Founder, iRecover Services, LLC

Raleigh, North Carolina

Michael.Hines@irecoverservices.com 919-215-3378 LinkedIn
“I am in recovery. That truth is not a footnote — it is the reason iRecover Services exists.”

I’ve lived the stigma, the employment gaps, and the quiet exhaustion of trying to rebuild a life while staying stable. I’ve also seen, from the inside, where the system breaks for people like me.

Before iRecover Services, the G.R.O.W.T.H. Program was launched in North Carolina — a workforce initiative serving individuals in recovery and reentry. Working alongside 20+ local employer partners, participants reached an 80% employment rate. That work revealed a deeper truth: people in recovery don’t just need access to jobs. They need an ecosystem of support, accountability, and stability.

National data tells the same story. Between 40–60% of people in early recovery relapse within the first year, and job loss is one of the strongest predictors. Staffing agencies stop at placement. Treatment programs stop at discharge. No one owns the 90-day window where lives and placements break down together.

iRecover Services closes that gap. This system integrates workforce coaching, peer mentorship, recovery-aware employer partnerships, housing and transportation support, and AI-driven retention analytics into one connected ecosystem — before the job, during the job, and after the job. A career in financial operations, compliance, risk, and AI systems taught what structure looks like. Recovery taught why it matters.

The mission is personal. The model is operational. The outcomes compound — for the individual, the employer, the community, and the economy. This is not charity. This is not luck. This is a system. And it works when it is built right.

— Michael Anthony Hines, Founder & CEO, iRecover Services, LLC

iRecover Services is currently enrolling Founding Employer Partners in Raleigh, NC. Be part of building something that has not existed before.

Apply for Pilot
Recovery, Re-imagined. Workforce, Rebuilt.

Why most recovery workforce programs fail.

Employment stability is one of the strongest predictors of long-term recovery outcomes. But employment alone has never been enough. iRecover Services exists to close exactly that gap.

The Problem

Instability is predictable.

Recovery is rarely derailed by a single moment — it unravels when work instability, unstable housing, and lost accountability compound. Employment stability is one of the strongest predictors of long-term recovery outcomes (SAMHSA).

The Gap

No one owns the middle.

Staffing agencies stop at the placement. Treatment programs stop at discharge. Neither system was built to hold the months in between — which is exactly when retention is decided.

Our Model

One connected ecosystem.

iRecover Services is the integrated ecosystem: workforce coaching, peer mentorship, employer consulting, housing and transportation support, and structured stability check-ins — operating as one connected system, not five disconnected services.

The Differentiator

Human-Led. AI-Powered. Life-Changing.

Real-time analytics surface the early warning signs that predict an exit. Every decision still runs through a person who understands recovery from the inside. Human-led, AI-powered, life-changing.

The Outcome

Retention compounds.

When the ecosystem holds, retention compounds. Employers gain a stable workforce and access to WOTC tax credits of $2,400–$9,600 per qualifying hire. Communities recover the talent recovery produces — and the math on workforce development quietly changes.

Which is why we built it the way we did — Before. During. After.

The Method

Before. During. After.

Three phases. One continuous system. Recovery is not a moment — it is a transition, supported at every step.

Before Readiness and stability
During Support and accountability
After Retention and long-term growth

Most systems stop at placement. We stay with the individual through the process.

B
Before
The Transition

Preparation Before Day 1

Readiness coaching, goal-setting, barrier planning, and accountability systems designed around recovery realities. Each person is matched to the right employer — not just the right job title.

D
During
The Integration

Support Through the First 90 Days

The first 90 days are when most placements fail. iRecover Services stays with both the candidate and the employer, catching early warning signs before they become exits.

A
After
The Stability

Retention, Growth, and What Comes Next

Sustained employment, financial independence, and reinforced recovery create momentum that extends into mentoring, leadership, and long-term community impact.

Staffing agencies stop at placement. Support programs stop at coaching.

iRecover Services is the ecosystem that holds both — and measures the result.

What We Do

Five Ways We Build Stability.

Every service connects directly to keeping someone employed — and keeping that employment sustainable. These are not separate services. They are the components of one ecosystem.

Recovery-Friendly Employers

Job Placement

Candidates are matched to employers prepared to support the workforce — not just tolerate it.

Preparation & Skills

Workforce Coaching

Confidence, gap analysis, and an employment plan that fits real life — before a single application goes out.

Human Connection

Peer Mentorship

Candidates are paired with mentors who understand accountability, encouragement, and the daily work of steadying a life while keeping work moving forward.

Workforce Performance

Employer Support

Recovery-aware hiring practices, WOTC tax credits of $2,400–$9,600 per qualifying hire, and culture that supports retention.

During & After Placement

Stability Check-ins

Structured touchpoints at every critical milestone catch early warning signs before they become exits — and keep employers informed.

The Platform Layer
Pathforge

We forge pathways from recovery to readiness to work.

More than services. A connected system.

Pathforge keeps people, employers, and support systems connected so progress doesn’t break down. It is the platform layer for recovery-to-workforce mobility — simple to experience, quietly powerful underneath.

01

Candidate Intelligence

Readiness tracking, stability signals, and support visibility — so no one falls through the cracks between stages.

02

Employer Integration

Job matching, retention insights, and workforce analytics that give employers a real view of what’s working.

03

Support Ecosystem

Coaches, peer mentors, transportation and housing signals — connected into one shared operating picture.

04

Outcomes Engine

30/60/90-day tracking, early risk detection, and intervention alerts that keep small issues from becoming exits.

We connect people, providers, employers, and outcomes data into one system — so recovery, work, and growth stay connected over time.

See Pathforge in a demo
The Ecosystem

One System. Every Stakeholder. Every Stage.

Recovery is fragile. Employment is fragile. The only thing strong enough to hold both is a system where every stakeholder — the person, the employer, the mentor, the coach, and the platform — works in coordination.

One system. Every stakeholder. Every stage.

Human-Led. AI-Powered. Life-Changing.

Who We Are

Recovery and Career Success
Go Hand in Hand.

Our Mission

iRecover Services empowers individuals in recovery to achieve long-term stability through a human-led, AI-powered workforce ecosystem — integrating job placement, coaching, training, housing, and real-time analytics to transform recovery into sustainable employment, retention, and lasting independence.

Our Vision

To become the national standard for recovery-centered workforce development — a scalable ecosystem where every individual in recovery has access to meaningful employment, personalized support, and the tools to build a thriving life, while employers, communities, and economies are strengthened by the talent recovery produces.

Recovery First

Every decision centers the person in recovery. Dignity and long-term success come before speed or volume.

Radical Inclusion

Barriers, records, gaps, and stigma are removed from the path between recovery-ready people and meaningful work.

Human-Led

Technology supports the work. People drive every decision. Dignity is never automated.

Accountable

Success is measured by whether people stay employed — not simply whether they were placed.

Where Recovery Meets Opportunity.

Stop Hiring.
Start Retaining.

iRecover Services is enrolling founding employer partners in Raleigh, NC. If you believe recovery and stable work belong together, we want to talk.

Hey, I’m Cecil — iRecover’s AI assistant. I’m here to help you take the next right step — whether that’s support, employment, or partnership.

Who are you today?

We put you first. Whether you’re just starting recovery or rebuilding your life, iRecover Services helps you find work that supports your stability — not disrupts it.

We stay with you before, during, and after employment. Where are you right now?

Build a workforce that stays. iRecover Services reduces turnover by supporting employees beyond placement — because most issues happen after hiring.

The BDA model (Before, During, After) keeps retention predictable, not reactive. Employers also access WOTC tax credits of $2,400–$9,600 per qualifying hire.

iRecover Services works with partners to create connected systems — not fragmented services. Treatment providers, community organizations, and workforce programs help build the continuity recovery needs.

Instead of handoffs, we build shared accountability.

← Back

Happy to help. A few quick things you might want to understand:

BDA — We support people Before they start working, During the critical first 90 days, and After placement. So they can stay employed — not just get hired.

Pathforge — the platform layer that keeps people, employers, and support systems connected so progress doesn’t break down.

← Back

You’re not alone. If you’re in crisis or need immediate support right now, please reach out to one of these:

Call or text 988 · Suicide & Crisis Lifeline Call 911 · Immediate danger SAMHSA Helpline · 1-800-662-HELP

iRecover Services is not a clinical or crisis provider. These resources are trained for exactly this moment.
